content.problemStatement:
This is the hard version of TOOFAREZ, and is worth 606060 points. Note that the input formats of both problems differ slightly - in particular, this version has an additional array AAA as input.

An array AAA of length MMM is said to be prefix-balanced under the following conditions:


Let SAS_ASA‚Äč denote the set of all elements that appear in AAA.
For each x‚ąąSAx \in S_Ax‚ąąSA‚Äč, and index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \leq i \leq M1‚Č§i‚Č§M), let fi(x)f_i(x)fi‚Äč(x) denote the number of times xxx appears among [A1,A2,‚Ä¶,Ai][A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i][A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai‚Äč].

That is, fi(x)f_i(x)fi‚Äč(x) denotes the frequency of xxx in AAA's prefix of length iii.
Then, AAA is called prefix-balanced if, for **every** triple of integers (x,y,i)(x, y, i)(x,y,i) such that x,y‚ąąSAx, y \in S_Ax,y‚ąąSA‚Äč and 1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \leq i \leq M1‚Č§i‚Č§M, we have

‚ą£fi(x)‚ąífi(y)‚ą£‚Č§1|f_i(x) - f_i(y)| \leq 1‚ą£fi‚Äč(x)‚ąífi‚Äč(y)‚ą£‚Č§1
For example, the arrays [1,2,1][1, 2, 1][1,2,1] and [1,3,2,3][1, 3, 2, 3][1,3,2,3] are prefix-balanced, but [2,3,2,4][2, 3, 2, 4][2,3,2,4] is not (the prefix of length 333 contains two occurrences of 222 and no occurrences of 444).

You are given an array AAA of length NNN, and an integer MMM.

Every element of AAA lies between 000 and MMM.

Let KKK denote the number of zeros in AAA. There are MKM^KMK arrays that can be obtained by replacing every 000 in AAA with an integer between 111 and MMM.

How many of these MKM^KMK arrays are prefix-balanced?

The answer might be large, so find it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the length of the array and the maximum allowed value.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of ways of replacing the zeros in AAA, such that the resulting array is prefix-balanced, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 5‚čÖ1055\cdot 10^55‚čÖ105.
